title_en: The Truth Is Still Out There
rubric_en: Why Americans remain convinced that the government is hiding an alien conspiracy
rubric_zh: 为什么美国人仍然坚信政府在隐瞒一场与外星人有关的阴谋

paragraphs.en: This article was featured in the One Story to Read Today newsletter. Sign up for it here.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章登载于 One Story to Read Today 每日精选通讯。点击这里订阅。

paragraphs.en: “There has been a threat to publicly release government material long shrouded in secrecy.” This sentence could have been intoned by a TV newscaster anytime in the past few years, about any number of real or alleged cover-ups—of Joe Biden’s mental decline, or the names in the Epstein files, or the origins of COVID-19. In fact, it comes from the trailer that aired during the Super Bowl for Disclosure Day, Steven Spielberg’s new movie, opening June 12. For people who believe in aliens, or who would like to be able to believe in them, that title leaves no doubt about the kinds of secrets in question: Disclosure refers to the long-awaited moment when the U.S. government will admit what it really knows about visitors to our planet.
paragraphs.zh: “长期被严密保密的政府材料即将被公开发布。”这句话在过去几年里，完全可能由电视新闻主播在任何时刻念出，谈论任何一种真实或所谓“被掩盖”的事件——无论是乔·拜登的精神状态下滑，还是爱泼斯坦档案中的姓名，抑或COVID-19的起源。事实上，这句话出自《Disclosure Day》的预告片。该片是史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格的新电影，计划于6月12日上映。对于相信外星人的人，或者希望自己能够相信外星人的人来说，这个片名毫不含糊地指明了其所涉秘密的性质：所谓“公开真相”指的是美国政府承认它真正了解的关于外星访客来访地球的事情的那一刻。

paragraphs.en: When President Trump promised, in a social-media post in February, “to begin the process of identifying and releasing Government files related to alien and extraterrestrial life,” he implied that disclosure might be just around the corner. It wasn’t: This morning, the Pentagon released a tranche of historic images on a new website, war.gov/ufo, which feature plenty of black-and-white murk but nothing that looks even a little like an alien spacecraft. Still, if history is any guide, this disappointment won’t put an end to the belief that the government is hiding a spaceship or an alien corpse; according to one of the best-known UFO legends, both were retrieved from a crash site near Roswell, New Mexico, in 1947. Or the proof could be something less tangible—a clear image of a nonhuman craft in flight, a radio signal from an extraterrestrial civilization. However it happens, disclosure will finally reveal the truth—not just about aliens, but about the authorities that have been deceiving us for so long.
paragraphs.zh: 当特朗普在2月的一条社交媒体帖子中承诺“启动识别并公开与外星生命和地外生命相关的政府档案”的进程时，他暗示这种“公开真相”或许就在眼前了。结果并非如此：今天早些时候，五角大楼在一个新网站 war.gov/ufo 上发布了一批历史图片，这些图片充满了黑白杂讯，却没有出现哪怕一点像外星飞船的影像。尽管如此，如果历史有任何参考意义，这种失望也不会结束人们相信政府正在隐瞒一艘飞船或一具外星人遗体的想法；按照最著名的UFO传说之一，这两样东西都于1947年在新墨西哥州罗斯威尔附近一处坠毁现场被找回。又或者所谓证据更不那么“有形”——比如一张清晰拍到非人类飞行器的照片，或来自外星文明的无线电讯号。无论怎样，公开真相最终都将揭示真相——不仅关于外星人，也关于那些长期以来一直在欺骗我们的当局。

paragraphs.en: This isn’t a new theme for science fiction, or for Spielberg. His career as a director took off in a post-Watergate climate when Hollywood was obsessed with official conspiracies and heroic whistleblowers—think of Alan J. Pakula’s All the President’s Men, Sydney Pollack’s Three Days of the Condor, and Francis Ford Coppola’s The Conversation. Spielberg brought this suspicious, anti-establishment mood to his early blockbusters, starting in 1975 with Jaws, in which the mayor of a northeastern beach town tries to cover up a deadly shark attack.
paragraphs.zh: 这并不是科幻小说中的新主题，也不是斯皮尔伯格才有的创作命题。作为导演的他在沃特门后时期崛起时，正值好莱坞沉迷于官方阴谋与英雄告密者叙事的阶段——想想艾伦·J·帕库拉的《林肯律师》、西德尼·波拉克的《秃鹰三天》、以及弗朗西斯·福特·科波拉的《窃听风暴》。斯皮尔伯格把这种多疑且反建制的基调带进了他的早期大片，最早可以追溯到1975年的《大白鲨》：一位东北部海滨小镇的市长试图掩盖一场致命的鲨鱼袭击。

paragraphs.en: Read: The blockbuster that captured a growing American rift
paragraphs.zh: 阅读：《捕捉美国日益加深撕裂的大片》

paragraphs.en: But the perfect genre for a story about government lies was the UFO movie, as Spielberg showed with Close Encounters of the Third Kind in 1977. “All I wanna do is know what’s going on,” says Roy Neary, the working-class hero played by Richard Dreyfuss, after a brush with a UFO. Unfortunately, powerful forces are determined to keep him from finding out. The term gaslighting wasn’t as popular then as it is today, but every military and government official in the movie is engaged in exactly that—trying to convince Neary, and other ordinary people like him, not to trust their own eyes.
paragraphs.zh: 但关于政府说谎的故事，最合适的类型恰恰是科幻片，就像斯皮尔伯格在1977年的《第三类接触》所展示的那样。‘我想知道的只是到底发生了什么，’理查德·德雷福斯饰演的工薪阶层英雄罗伊·尼里在与不明飞行物接触后说道。不幸的是，强大的势力决意让他无法知道真相。‘煤气灯效应’这个词当时并没有今天这么流行，但片中的每一位军方和政府官员都在做同样的事——试图说服尼里，以及和他一样的普通人，不要相信自己眼睛所见。

paragraphs.en: “Now, there are all kinds of ideas that would be fun to believe in—mental telepathy, time travel, immortality, even Santa Claus,” a condescending government spokesman says to a group of UFO witnesses. At the film’s climax, the Army invents a story about a chemical-weapons spill as an excuse for evacuating a swath of Wyoming where the aliens are expected to land. If they hadn’t finally shown themselves at the end of the movie—in a sky-filling, strobe-lit mother ship too awe-inspiring to conceal—there’s no doubt the U.S. government would have gone on hiding the truth forever.
paragraphs.zh: ‘现在有很多有意思的说法可以信，譬如心灵感应、时间旅行、永生，甚至圣诞老人。’一名居高临下的政府发言人对一群UFO目击者这么说。影片高潮时，军方编造了一个化学武器泄漏的故事，作为撤离怀俄明州一片即将接待外星人降落地带的借口。如果他们在电影结尾没有露面——那艘占据天空、被频闪灯照亮、令人震撼到几乎无法掩饰存在的母舰——那美国政府肯定会继续把真相永远藏起来。

paragraphs.en: That’s just what it tries to do in Spielberg’s next alien movie, E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial, from 1982. When the government learns that Elliott, a young boy, is hiding an adorable alien, his entire house is sealed off in a plastic tarp—a quarantine that also serves as a perfect concealment. When the boy and the alien manage to break out, they are pursued by agents with guns, escaping with the aid of a bicycle and the power of imagination.
paragraphs.zh: 这正是斯皮尔伯格1982年的下一部外星题材电影《E.T. 外星人》所再度尝试的做法。影片中，当政府发现小男孩艾利特在掩护一位可爱的外星人时，他们用塑料防护膜将他全家的房子封锁起来——这场隔离措施同时也是一场完美的掩护。当男孩和外星人成功逃脱时，便遭到持枪特工的追捕；而最后，正是在一辆自行车以及想象力的力量帮助下，他们才得以逃离。

paragraphs.en: The lesson of these movies is clear: Trust yourself, not the government. It’s a message deeply in the American grain, and science fiction has been amplifying it for decades. In the long-running TV series The X-Files, the FBI agents Mulder and Scully battle the “Syndicate,” a conspiracy at the highest levels of power to sell out the human race to alien invaders. The Men in Black movies play the idea for laughs, imagining a world where law enforcement keeps tabs on aliens living among us in disguise. The titular agents use a “neuralyzer” device to wipe the memory of anyone who stumbles upon the secret.
paragraphs.zh: 这些电影的核心教训很明确：相信自己，而不是政府。这是美国人性格中的一种深层气质，科幻电影数十年来一直在不断强化它。在长期播出的电视剧《X档案》中，联邦调查局探员麦尔德与斯库利与“辛迪加”作斗争——这是一个盘踞在最高权力层面的阴谋组织，企图把整个人类族群出卖给外星入侵者。《黑衣人》系列则把这一想法做成喜剧化处理，想象一个执法体系在监控伪装成普通人生活在我们之中的外星人。片中的两位主角会用一种“神经清除器”装置抹去任何偶然发现秘密的人的记忆。

paragraphs.en: Disclosure Day promises a new kind of UFO story, in which government secrecy is defeated and the world finally learns the truth. Spielberg may be a half century older than when he made Close Encounters, but he clearly hasn’t lost his power to read the mood of American culture. In the past decade, a profound shift has taken place in the way we talk and think about UFOs. To quote the title of a 2025 documentary on the subject, we are living in “The Age of Disclosure.”
paragraphs.zh: 《披露日》承诺带来一种全新的UFO叙事：政府的保密被击败，世界最终得知真相。史蒂文·斯皮尔伯格虽然比拍摄《第三类接触》时年纪大了约半个世纪，但他显然并未失去把握美国文化情绪的能力。过去十年里，我们谈论和思考UFO的方式发生了深刻变化。正如一部2025年相关纪录片的标题所说，我们正生活在“披露时代”。

paragraphs.en: The most important sign of this change is that aliens have become respectable. It used to be that only supermarket tabloids such as the National Enquirer reported on UFO sightings; now they are seriously discussed in mainstream media and congressional hearings. Even the term UFO has fallen out of favor, tainted by its long association with crankery. Government officials and true believers alike now prefer to talk about UAP. At first the acronym stood for “unidentified aerial phenomena,” but aerial was soon changed to anomalous, to include all kinds of “space, airborne, submerged, and transmedium objects.” That is how UAP are defined in the mission statement of the All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, a government agency created in 2022 “to synchronize efforts across the Department of Defense, and with other U.S. federal departments and agencies, to detect, identify and attribute objects of interest.”
paragraphs.zh: 这一变化最重要的标志是，外星人议题变得“体面”了。过去只有《国家内幕报》这类超市八卦小报会报道UFO目击事件；如今，它们已在主流媒体和国会听证会上被严肃讨论。连“UFO”这个词本身也不再受欢迎了，因为它长期以来与异想怪论纠缠在一起。政府官员和坚定的信徒如今都更愿意使用“UAP”这一说法。起初该缩写指的是“未识别空中现象”（unidentified aerial phenomena），但很快“空中”一词被改为“异常”，以便涵盖各种“太空、空中、水下和跨介质物体”。这就是“全域异常解释办公室”（All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office）在其任务声明中的定义；该政府机构于2022年成立，目标是“协调国防部与其他美国联邦部门和机构的努力，识别、鉴别并归因值得关注的目标。”

paragraphs.en: Our age of disclosure was born on December 16, 2017, when The New York Times published an article headlined “Glowing Auras and ‘Black Money’: The Pentagon’s Mysterious U.F.O. Program.” The story revealed that, from 2007 to 2012, the Defense Department had allocated approximately $22 million to the Advanced Aerospace Threat Identification Program (AATIP), a secret task force charged with investigating reports of flying objects that maneuver and accelerate in ways that ordinary aircraft cannot.
paragraphs.zh: 我们的披露时代始于2017年12月16日，那天《纽约时报》发表了题为“发光灵气与‘黑色资金’：五角大楼神秘的U.F.O.计划”的文章。该报道披露，在2007年至2012年间，国防部向“先进航空威胁识别计划”（AATIP）拨款约2200万美元，这是一个秘密任务组，负责调查那些运动轨迹和加速方式都非普通飞机所能做到的飞行物报告。

paragraphs.en: Such sightings aren’t new. Americans have been noticing inexplicable things in the sky since World War II, when pilots over Germany reported being followed by glowing balls that they nicknamed “foo fighters.” The term unidentified flying object was coined in the early 1950s to describe such phenomena in a neutral, noncommittal fashion. But of course, what made UFOs fascinating was the possibility that they could be extraterrestrial spacecraft.
paragraphs.zh: 这些目击并不新鲜。美国人在二战以来就开始注意到天空中无法解释的现象，当时飞行员在德国上空报告说自己被一些发光球体跟踪，他们把这些现象称作“妖火（foo fighters）”。“不明飞行物”这一术语在20世纪50年代初被提出，用来中性、含糊地称呼这类现象。然而，人们之所以被UFO吸引，当然还因为它们可能是外星飞船。

paragraphs.en: In 1966, public concern about the issue prompted the Air Force to convene a panel of scientists to review UFO reports. The committee, headed by the physicist Edward Condon, bluntly concluded that such sightings were meaningless, blaming them on “inexperienced, inept, or unduly excited” observers who mistake ordinary sights like planets and balloons for flying saucers. “Nothing has come from the study of UFOs in the past 21 years that has added to scientific knowledge,” the committee reported, and “further extensive study of UFOs probably cannot be justified.”
paragraphs.zh: 1966年，公众对此问题的担忧促使空军召集一组科学家委员会来审查UFO报告。该委员会由物理学家爱德华·康登（Edward Condon）主持，赤裸直言地得出结论：这些目击毫无意义，归因于“经验不足、能力不够，或过度兴奋”的观察者，把行星和气球等普通景象误认为飞碟。委员会报告称：“在过去21年对UFO的研究没有产生任何可补充科学知识的成果，”并且“进一步对UFO进行广泛研究可能是不能被证明必要的。”

paragraphs.en: That verdict led the U.S. military to stop officially taking notice of sightings, even by its own pilots. But the 2017 revelations about AATIP seemed to prove what advocates of disclosure had always maintained: Though the Pentagon publicly denied that it had any evidence of aliens, it actually knew they were real. In fact, it possessed videos of encounters between UAP and American military aircraft. The Times and other news outlets published several of these videos online—low-resolution black-and-white footage of what looked like a small blob zooming over the ocean.
paragraphs.zh: 这一裁决促使美军停止官方关注此类目击事件，甚至不再重视自己的飞行员报告。可到2017年，关于AATIP的披露似乎证明了长期主张公开透明的倡导者一直坚持的观点：尽管五角大楼公开否认拥有任何外星人证据，它实际上知道它们是真实存在的。事实上，它掌握了美国军机与UAP接触的影像。美国《时代》杂志及其他媒体随后在网上发布了这些影像中的几段——低清晰度的黑白片段，显示的是似乎在海面上空高速掠过的一个小斑点。

paragraphs.en: All of this respectful attention drove a transformation in public opinion. In 1996, a Newsweek poll found that 20 percent of Americans believed that UFOs were “probably alien ships or alien life forms.” When a YouGov poll asked the same question in 2022, that figure had increased to 34 percent. Even people who don’t think aliens have been here are now much more likely to believe that they will be here soon. In 1996, 69 percent of Americans thought that humanity would not contact aliens in the next half century; by 2022, only 39 percent did.
paragraphs.zh: 所有这些“受重视的”关注推动了公众舆论的变化。1996年，《新闻周刊》的一项民调显示，20%的美国人相信UFO“可能是外星飞船或外星生命体”。当YouGov在2022年提出同一问题时，这一数字上升到34%。即使是不认为外星人已经到达地球的人，如今也更容易相信外星人很快就会到来。1996年，69%的美国人认为人类在未来半个世纪内不会与外星人取得联系；到2022年，这一比例降至39%。

paragraphs.en: No wonder politicians who would once have scoffed at UFOs began to see them as a winning issue. Disclosure has never seemed closer than it did on July 26, 2023, when the House Oversight Committee held an open hearing on UAP as a national-security threat. Witnesses with apparently unimpeachable credentials testified under oath that the U.S. military has been hiding its knowledge of UFOs for decades. David Fravor, a retired Navy pilot, said that in 2004, his fighter squadron encountered a “white Tic Tac object” in the sky off the coast of San Diego. The craft had “no rotors, no rotor wash, or any sign of visible control surfaces like wings,” yet it was able to outrun fighter jets.
paragraphs.zh: 难怪那些曾经会嘲笑“不明飞行物”的政治人物，如今开始把它当作一项能够赢得支持的议题。披露问题在 2023 年 7 月 26 日以前从未看似如此接近：那一天，众议院监督委员会举行了一场公开听证会，将 UAP 作为国家安全威胁进行讨论。几位表面上资历不可动摇的证人作证发誓，美国军方几十年来一直在隐瞒其关于 UFO 的相关认知。退役海军飞行员戴维·弗拉沃（David Fravor）说，在 2004 年，他所在的战斗机中队在圣地亚哥海岸上空遇到一个“白色的蒂卡克（Tic Tac）状物体”。该器械“没有旋翼、没有螺旋桨的气流痕迹，也看不到任何可见的控制面，比如机翼”，但它却能够超过战斗机的速度。

paragraphs.en: David Grusch, a former Air Force intelligence officer, made even more explosive claims. “I was informed in the course of my official duties of a multi-decade UAP-crash retrieval and reverse-engineering program” that operated in secret, “above congressional oversight,” Grusch testified. In interviews with journalists, he was more explicit, saying that the U.S. government possessed both intact spacecraft and extraterrestrial bodies.
paragraphs.zh: 前空军情报官大卫·格鲁什（David Grusch）则提出了更具爆炸性的说法。格鲁什在作证时称：“在我的公务职责过程中，我被告知存在一个跨越数十年的 UAP 撞击回收与逆向工程项目”——该项目秘密运行，“高于国会监督”。在接受媒体采访时，他说得更明确，称美国政府既掌握着完整的飞行器，也掌握着外星生物遗体。

paragraphs.en: Last year’s documentary The Age of Disclosure includes similar claims. Pilots talk about seeing objects with no wings or engines that seemed to defy the laws of physics, or at least the limits of human technology; Fravor mentions an oval-shaped object that could move at speeds of “32,000 miles an hour.” The narrator of the film, Luis Elizondo—a former Army intelligence officer who worked on AATIP—talks about a Defense Department effort he calls the “Legacy Program,” which has “been capturing, retrieving, and reverse engineering UAPs since at least 1947. On numerous occasions, these retrievals included the bodies of nonhumans.”
paragraphs.zh: 去年的纪录片《The Age of Disclosure》也包含了类似说法。飞行员们谈到自己看到过没有机翼和发动机、似乎违背物理定律——或者至少超越人类技术极限——的目标；弗拉沃提到过一个椭圆形物体，其速度可以达到“每小时 32,000 英里”。影片的旁白是路易斯·埃利松多（Luis Elizondo），他是曾参与 AATIP 的前陆军情报官，讲到美国国防部一项他称为“遗产计划（Legacy Program）”的行动——“至少从 1947 年起就在捕获、回收并对 UAP 进行逆向工程”。他表示，“在许多场合，这些回收中都包括了非人类生物的遗体。”

paragraphs.en: These are exactly the kinds of admissions that disclosure was supposed to bring, and though the more outlandish claims were denied by the government and treated skeptically by the mainstream media, they couldn’t be ignored—not when they were taken seriously by people such as Secretary of State Marco Rubio and former Director of National Intelligence James Clapper, both of whom appear in The Age of Disclosure. Who could blame UFO believers for thinking that the world was about to change in profound and disconcerting ways? “This is Disclosure. This is it Right Now,” a Reddit user announced on r/UFOs, a forum with hundreds of thousands of weekly visitors, in the aftermath of the House hearings. “If you have loved ones, it may be a good idea to begin deciding how you will broach the subject, especially if they are dependents.”
paragraphs.zh: 这正是披露本应带来的那种“认罪式”承认。尽管政府否认了更离谱的说法，而主流媒体也对它们持怀疑态度，但这些内容仍然无法被轻易忽视——因为它们被国务卿马克·鲁比奥（Marco Rubio）和前国家情报局长詹姆斯·克拉珀（James Clapper）等人认真对待，而这两位都出现在《The Age of Disclosure》里。谁能责怪 UFO 信仰者认为世界正要以深刻且令人不安的方式发生变化呢？“这就是披露。这就是现在。”一位 Reddit 用户在众议院听证会后，在每周有数十万访客的 r/UFOs 论坛上宣布道，“如果你有家人，也许现在是个该开始想好如何开口提起这个话题的时候了，尤其是当对方是未成年人时。”

paragraphs.en: In the Disclosure Day trailer, one character asks what would happen “if you found we weren’t alone. If someone showed you, proved it to you.” But showing and proving are exactly what Elizondo and other self-styled whistleblowers have never been able to do. For all the attention paid to UFOs over the past decade, we still have no evidence that they exist. The public may be more willing to listen to claims about downed spaceships and alien life forms, but we haven’t actually seen any. The murky Pentagon UFO videos have not been followed by clear pictures of alien spacecraft, which could theoretically be taken by anyone with an iPhone.
paragraphs.zh: 在《Disclosure Day》预告片中，一个角色问道：“如果你发现我们并不孤独，会发生什么？如果有人向你展示，向你证明了这一点呢？”但“展示”并“证明”正是埃利松多（Elizondo）以及其他自封吹哨人一直做不到的。尽管过去十年里人们对不明飞行物（UFO）关注度很高，我们仍然没有任何证据表明它们确实存在。公众可能更愿意听到关于坠毁飞船和外星生命形式的说法，但我们实际上还没见过任何东西。令人困惑的五角大楼 UFO 视频，也没有带来任何清晰的外星飞船画面；按理说，这些画面理论上任何一部 iPhone 都能拍到。

paragraphs.en: The most parsimonious explanation for this failure is that there is nothing to disclose. But UFO believers are compelled to reject this idea, because the U.S. government is better equipped than any entity on Earth to detect the arrival of extraterrestrials. If they have been here, some kind of cover-up is a logical necessity. At least one person is certain enough to bet on it: In February, the prediction market Kalshi recorded two wagers, totaling almost $300,000, that the U.S. government would announce the existence of aliens by the end of the year. Inevitably, the news prompted speculation that the bettor was a Trump-administration insider who knows that something big is coming.
paragraphs.zh: 对这种失败最省思的解释是：根本没有什么可披露的。但 UFO 信奉者会被迫否认真相，因为美国政府比地球上任何实体都更有能力侦测到地外生物的到来。如果他们真的在这里，某种程度上的掩盖就是合乎逻辑的必然。至少有一个人足够确信，以至于押上了赌注：在二月，预测市场 Kalshi 记录了两笔赌注，总额接近 30 万美元，赌美国政府到年底前将宣布外星人存在。新闻不出所料地引发了猜测，认为这位投注者是熟悉内幕的特朗普政府内部人士，他知道将会有大事发生。

paragraphs.en: Read: This looks like an insider bet on aliens
paragraphs.zh: 相关阅读：这看起来像是一次内部人士对外星人的押注

paragraphs.en: Disclosure isn’t just about logic, however. It is awaited with an almost religious fervor because it will give UFO believers the same kind of affirmation that the coming of the Messiah will give religious believers. Faith, the New Testament says, is the evidence of things not seen. But at the end of days, when God finally becomes visible, faith will give way to knowledge: “For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face.” UFOs are not supposed to be supernatural; if they exist, they must obey the same laws of physics that reign here on Earth. But for now, they remain objects of faith because we have never been able to see one face-to-face. Disclosure will show that this faith was justified all along—that the believers were right and the skeptics wrong.
paragraphs.zh: “公开披露”不仅仅是逻辑问题，它还被一种近乎宗教般的热忱所期待，因为它会让 UFO 信徒获得与宗教信徒期待弥赛亚降临时相同的确认。新约说，信心是对未见之事的证成。但在末日将临之际，当上帝终将显现时，信心就会让位于知识：“如今我们仿佛对着一面蒙灰的镜子观看，到那时却要面对面了。” UFO 并不应该被视为超自然现象；如果它们存在，就必须遵循地球上同样的物理法则。然而现在，它们仍然是“信仰”的对象，因为我们从未能与其“面对面”见过。公开披露将证明这种信仰从一开始就是有道理的——相信者是对的，怀疑者是错的。

paragraphs.en: If this sounds a bit like a revenge fantasy, that’s understandable. UFO belief, like traditional religion, tends to attract the scorn of what the Protestant theologian Friedrich Schleiermacher called “cultured despisers,” people who consider themselves too sophisticated to fall for a popular error. How sweet it will be when disclosure proves that the experts and elites were wrong—worse, that they were actively suppressing the truth that nonexperts always knew was out there.
paragraphs.zh: 如果这听起来有些像一场复仇式幻想，那也不难理解。像 UFO 信仰一样，传统宗教也常常招致新教神学家弗里德里希·施莱尔马赫所说“受过教养的轻蔑者”的嘲讽，他们自认过于精明，不会上当于大众的错误观念。当专家和精英被证明是错的时；更糟的是，他们实际上是在压制那些非专家早已知道却始终存在的真相，这种时刻会有多让人心情舒畅。

paragraphs.en: This dynamic of condescension and vindication has become central to American life over the past decade. It drives all kinds of populist causes: vaccine skepticism, the MAHA movement, Roswell-level conspiracy theories such as QAnon and Pizzagate. Every kind of “truther” makes a demand for disclosure—to stop hiding the truth about why the World Trade Center collapsed on 9/11, or where Barack Obama was born, or how Trump’s ear got bloodied in Butler, Pennsylvania.
paragraphs.zh: 这种居高临下与等待洗刷的心理，在过去十年已成为美国社会生活的核心。它推动了各种民粹主义议题：疫苗怀疑、MAHA 运动、以及像 QAnon 和 Pizzagate 这样的罗斯威尔级阴谋论。各种“真相揭露者”都在发出同一种要求——要求公开真相，不要再掩盖为什么世贸中心在 911 事件中倒塌、巴拉克·奥巴马出生地到底在哪里、或者特朗普在宾夕法尼亚州巴特勒为何耳部受伤流血。

paragraphs.en: From the June 2020 issue: Adrienne LaFrance on a dangerous new phase of American conspiracism
paragraphs.zh: 摘自2020年6月刊：Adrienne LaFrance 论美国阴谋论的新危险阶段

paragraphs.en: It’s tempting to dismiss these as fantasies born of ignorance and nourished by paranoia. And the cultured despisers are right, most of the time. There is no convincing evidence that the Earth has been visited by aliens, just as there is no convincing evidence that vaccines cause autism. And yet, the Pentagon really was hiding videos of flying objects that could not be readily explained. Jeffrey Epstein really was friendly with royals and presidents. These things were disclosed only after years of public pressure from people who weren’t content with the official story.
paragraphs.zh: 人们很容易把这些解释为无知中滋生、由偏执滋长的幻想，而“文化人蔑视者”在大多数时候也确实是对的。没有令人信服的证据表明地球曾被外星人访问，就像没有令人信服的证据证明疫苗会导致自闭症一样。然而，美国国防部确实曾秘密保存过无法轻易解释的飞行物体影像。杰弗里·爱泼斯坦确实与王室和总统关系密切。这些情况也都在长期以来，一些不满足于官方说法的民众持续施压后才被公开。

paragraphs.en: And when people are convinced that they know a secret, world-shaking truth, they are willing to wait a long time for vindication. This is another way in which UFO disclosure resembles the coming of the Messiah: Both are constantly running behind schedule.
paragraphs.zh: 当人们确信自己掌握了一个关于世界真相的秘密时，他们愿意为了得到证明而耐心等待很长时间。这是飞碟公开这一事宜与救世主降临预言相似的另一点：两者都总是一直“延后”到来。

paragraphs.en: In 1950, Donald E. Keyhoe, a pilot and fiction writer, published a book called The Flying Saucers Are Real, in which he argued that the government’s apparent UFO denials were actually “part of an elaborate program to prepare the American people for a dramatic disclosure.” After all, the news that there are aliens among us would likely have devastating consequences. People would panic about a possible invasion of the planet and turn against institutions that had been hiding the truth. Nations would compete to benefit from the newcomers and their technology, and religious authorities would have to rethink the foundations of their faith.
paragraphs.zh: 1950年，飞行员兼科幻作家唐纳德·E·凯霍发表了《飞碟真实存在》一书，其中他认为政府对不明飞行物的公开否认其实是“精心策划的一场让美国人民为重大公开真相做好准备的计划”的一部分。毕竟，关于外星人就在我们中间的消息可能会带来毁灭性后果。人们会因可能的地球入侵而恐慌，并转而反对那些一直隐瞒真相的机构。各国将争相从新来者及其技术中获利，宗教当局也将不得不重新思考信仰的根基。

paragraphs.en: It makes sense that the custodians of such knowledge would want to release it little by little, to help humanity prepare for the shock. In fact, UFO believers have long speculated that Hollywood stories about aliens play a role in this acclimatization process. Some online theorists are already arguing that Disclosure Day itself is part of such a campaign: “Is this all just coincidence + perfect marketing timing for the movie? Or has someone been dropping clues?” one Redditor asked after the first trailer appeared.
paragraphs.zh: 由此可见，掌握这类知识的人会愿意分阶段释放消息，以便让人类逐步适应这次冲击，这一点是说得通的。事实上，UFO 信徒早已猜测，关于外星人的好莱坞故事在这个“适应过程”中也起到作用。一些网络理论者已经开始辩称，所谓的“公开日”本身就是这类活动的一部分。第一支预告片发布后，一位 Reddit 用户问道：“这会不会只是巧合加上电影的完美营销时机？还是有人一直在放线索？”

paragraphs.en: Keyhoe promised that “the official explanation may be imminent.” In 2024, Elizondo used virtually identical language in his book, Imminent: Inside the Pentagon’s Hunt for UFOs, looking forward to “imminent government disclosure about nonhuman intelligence.” Three-quarters of a century is a long time for disclosure to remain imminent. But if it proves once and for all that humanity is not alone in the universe, isn’t it worth the wait? Religious believers have been waiting thousands of years for the apocalypse, the vision of the End Times described in the biblical books of Daniel and Revelation. Apocalypse and disclosure are, in fact, Greek and Latin ways of saying the same thing: Both refer to uncovering, the removal of concealment. And as long as we’re convinced that some great, mysterious truth is being hidden, we don’t have to confront an even more unsettling possibility—that there’s nothing out there to believe in at all.
paragraphs.zh: 凯霍曾承诺“官方解释可能迫在眉睫”。到了2024年，埃利桑多在《迫在眉睫：五角大楼追寻不明飞行物内幕》一书中用几乎同样的话语展望“关于非人类智能的政府公开很快就要来临”。让“公开”持续悬而未决三分之四个世纪，是一段漫长的等待。然而，如果它最终能彻底证明人类并非宇宙中独自一族，这种等待是否值得？有宗教信仰者已经等待了几千年，等候圣经《但以理书》和《启示录》所描写的末世降临景象。实际上，“末世”与“公开”在希腊语和拉丁语里都指向同一件事：揭示、去除隐藏。如果我们始终确信有某个伟大而神秘的真相被掩盖着，我们就不用面对一个更令人不安的可能性——也许根本没有值得相信的东西存在。

paragraphs.en: This article appears in the June 2026 print edition with the headline “Alien Nation.” When you buy a book using a link on this page, we receive a commission. Thank you for supporting The Atlantic.
paragraphs.zh: 这篇文章刊登在 2026 年 6 月的纸质版中，标题为“外星国”。当你通过本页链接购买一本书时，我们会获得一笔佣金。感谢你对《大西洋》的支持。
